explanation: The command substitutes the long option '--interface' with the short option '-i' and '--port' with '-p', which are equivalent in many command-line tools. This is a valid mutation under substitution rules.
The command removes the space between the flag and its value, which is a common way to pass arguments in command-line interfaces. This is a valid mutation under omission rules.
The command uses the equals sign (=) to separate the option and its value, which is a valid syntax in many command-line tools. This is a valid mutation under substitution rules.
The command reorders the arguments '-i' and '-p'. If the program accepts arguments in any order, this is a valid mutation under reordering rules.
The command adds quotes around the values, which is a valid way to pass arguments in command-line interfaces. This is a valid mutation under insertion rules.
